serverNeeds.description: Single-definition schema coverage metric (covered pages / total, with definition of covered = published vs generated per Q5), serialized onto the snapshot view so the coverage ring + summary strip and any Site Audit/Links reuse read one number.
serverNeeds.evidence: grep 'coverage' across server/domains/schema + server/schema*.ts hits only workflow-guide copy in src/components/schema/SchemaWorkflowGuide.tsx; toAdminSchemaSnapshotView (server/routes/webflow-schema.ts:110-115) returns pages+publishDates but no aggregate.
serverNeeds.whereItLives: server/domains/schema/ new coverage helper, surfaced via toAdminSchemaSnapshotView (server/audit-snapshot-views.ts if shared with audit)

serverNeeds.description: 'Missing schema — from Site Audit' count: expose a per-workspace count of audit schema findings consumable by the Schema surface. NOTE: the check ids the frontend expects (missing_schema/schema_errors) have NO server producer — server emits only aeo-faq-no-schema; the finding itself must be defined or the frontend mapping fixed first.
serverNeeds.evidence: grep -rc 'missing_schema' server shared = 0 occurrences (only src/components/SeoAudit.tsx:547 + src/components/audit/types.ts:98 reference it); server producer emits 'aeo-faq-no-schema' (server/audit-page.ts:384).
serverNeeds.whereItLives: server audit domain (server/audit-page.ts check producer + a snapshot/coverage projection), possibly folded into sn-schema-1

serverNeeds.description: Insights Engine graduation pipeline: schema deploys that win a rich result / AI citation graduate into a client win. Producer half exists (idempotent outcome recordAction on publish); the detection + graduation consumer does not.
serverNeeds.evidence: server/domains/schema/publish-schema-to-live.ts:108-136 records outcome action + GSC baseline; grep for any graduation/insight producer from schema outcomes came up empty (no bridge in server/intelligence or insight stores references schema wins).
serverNeeds.whereItLives: server insight-bridge module (bridge-authoring contract) consuming outcome-measurement results

openQuestions.question: Q1 — Schema Site Plan subsystem (rows 47-55) has no home in the prototype: bridge-mount, redesign, or retire?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Bridge-mount existing SchemaPlanPanel inside the rebuilt surface (ratified T1 carry-over-then-reskin default); redesign as a follow-up.

openQuestions.question: Q2 — CMS-field publishing pipeline home (rows 35-36, 58): same-surface advanced panel, settings-level config, or sequenced later?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Same-surface advanced panel (Drawer/GroupBlock), sequenced within one release train so CMS-heavy sites never lose publish capability.

openQuestions.question: Q3 — Confirm all five publish-safety affordances carry (graph gate, manual-delivery fallback, retract, history/rollback, site template).
openQuestions.proposedDefault: All carry — forced by the no-capability-deletion hard floor; only the UI form is discretionary.

openQuestions.question: Q4 — Does Insights Engine graduation REPLACE or COMPLEMENT the GSC before/after impact panel?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Complement: carry the aggregate impact panel; graduation (sn-schema-3) is a C3-style addition.

openQuestions.question: Q5 — Coverage % definition: existing vs generated vs published (mockup is internally inconsistent).
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Published-live over total pages (matches 'live in Webflow' copy); server-computed per sn-schema-1.

openQuestions.question: Q6 — 'Missing schema — from Site Audit' stat scope + owner (data ticket).
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Fold into sn-schema-1/2 shared coverage service; ship the strip without this stat until the ticket lands.

openQuestions.question: Q7 — Surface the dark competitor-schema endpoint in the rebuild or leave internal-only?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Leave internal-only (feeds plan generation); revisit with the Competitors surface.

openQuestions.question: Q8 — Retire orphaned SchemaHealthDashboard + SchemaReviewTab/Modal in the rebuild?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Retire with DEF-* ledger rows; keep the shared respondToSchemaPlanFeedback service intact.

openQuestions.question: Q9 — Prototype styles Schema purple; HEAD is teal.
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Keep teal — Phase D standing policy says HEAD no-purple is the default and Badge purple/tone is a Tier-2 F2/F3 decision.

openQuestions.question: NEW — Frontend audit check ids missing_schema/schema_errors have no server producer (server emits aeo-faq-no-schema): is SeoAudit.tsx:547's hasSchemaIssues tip dead at HEAD, and which check taxonomy should sn-schema-2 build on?
openQuestions.proposedDefault: Treat as a pre-existing wiring bug; define the schema checks server-side as part of sn-schema-2 and fix the frontend mapping.

verdicts.evidence: server/serializers/client-safe.ts:213-227 (toAdminSchemaSnapshotView returns id/siteId/workspaceId/createdAt/pageCount/results only — no aggregate); rg 'coverage' across server/domains/schema + server/routes/webflow-schema.ts + server/schema*.ts = 0 real hits; server/schema-store.ts / schema-plan.ts have no covered/total metric.
verdicts.correction: Core claim STANDS: no single-definition schema coverage metric (covered/total) exists on any read path — confirmed. But the gatherer's evidence file:line is WRONG. toAdminSchemaSnapshotView is NOT at server/routes/webflow-schema.ts:110-115 (that line just CALLS it) — it is DEFINED at server/serializers/client-safe.ts:213. The 'whereItLives' should point there (not server/audit-snapshot-views.ts, which exists but is the audit-side twin and does not serialize schema). Effort S holds: covered/total is derivable from the existing snapshot.results[] + publishDates already passed to the serializer, so it is a computed helper + one field on AdminSchemaSnapshotView — no migration, no DB-column lockstep.

verdicts.evidence: server/audit-page.ts emits only 'structured-data' (info, line 180: 'No structured data (JSON-LD) found') and 'aeo-faq-no-schema' (warning, line 384) — grep of all `check: '...'` producers confirms NO 'missing_schema' or 'schema_errors' producer anywhere in server/ or shared/. Frontend consumers reference the phantom ids: src/components/SeoAudit.tsx:547 (hasSchemaIssues checks ['missing_schema','schema_errors']) and src/components/audit/types.ts:98 ('missing_schema':'seo-schema','schema_errors':'seo-schema'). The frontend→server mapping is broken as described.
verdicts.correction: existsToday=no is correct; effort M is defensible — the finding must be defined server-side (new check producer in audit-page.ts) OR the frontend mapping repointed to the real 'structured-data'/'aeo-faq-no-schema' ids, then a per-workspace count projection built. Note the frontend already has a working 'structured-data'/AEO signal it is not using; the cheapest fix is repoint-frontend, but a proper 'missing schema' count still needs a producer + projection.

verdicts.evidence: Producer confirmed: server/domains/schema/publish-schema-to-live.ts:113-135 records a 'schema_deployed' action with baseline {rich_result_eligible:true, rich_result_appearing:false}. Measurement consumer EXISTS (gatherer missed it): server/outcome-measurement.ts:329,552 scores schema_deployed (primary_metric='ctr', clicks×CPC dollar attribution); server/workspace-learnings.ts:410-417 aggregates schemaWins filtered on outcome.metricsSnapshot.rich_result_appearing===true into schemaTypesWithRichResults. DETECTION is the true gap: fetchCurrentMetrics (outcome-measurement.ts:128-145) only reads GSC rows via averageGscRows — no path ever sets rich_result_appearing=true, so the workspace-learnings:414 filter is dead. GRADUATION bridge absent: rg over server/insight*/server/intelligence/server/domains/recommendations for schema_deployed/schema-win bridges = 0 hits (bridge inventory: bridge-local-visibility-shift, webflow-seo-audit-bridges — neither touches schema outcomes).
verdicts.correction: existsToday=partial is right, but the partition is wrong. The claim frames 'detection + graduation consumer' as one missing block; in fact an OUTCOME-MEASUREMENT/SCORING consumer already exists and dollar-attributes schema deploys — that is NOT net-new work. What is genuinely absent: (1) rich-result / AI-citation DETECTION that flips rich_result_appearing:false→true (GSC alone cannot supply this — a net-new data source, the real cost driver), and (2) the insight-bridge that graduates a detected schema win into a client-facing win/insight. Effort L holds precisely because of the missing detection data source, not because scoring must be built. Plan the L against 'detection source + bridge', and reuse the existing outcome-measurement + workspace-learnings machinery rather than rebuilding it.
